title: Pool Status (fixed-size golem worker pool)
description: Operator policy + display cache for the orchestrate worker pool, written to .worktrees/.status/pool.json. AUTHORITATIVE for operator policy (pool `size` and `accepting` state — the one place that information lives); the `slots`/`backlog_depth`/`updated` fields are a DISPLAY CACHE only. PR/MR state and issue status labels remain authoritative for golem liveness. NOT a golem-status file — `just golems` excludes pool.json from its golem-row glob and reads it separately for the pool header. Sibling to golem-status.schema.json and agent-status.schema.json.

size.description: Target number of concurrent golems the pool maintains. Changed live via `pool <N>`: grow fills free slots now; shrink lets the excess golems drain (never killed).

accepting.description: Refill policy. 'accepting' = refill a free slot from the backlog. 'draining' = stop refills and let in-flight golems finish to idle (one-way wind-down intent — for context reset / service restart / end of day). 'paused' = freeze refills without draining (slots held, resumable via `resume`).

slots.description: In-flight slot -> issue map (DISPLAY CACHE; the live golem set is authoritative via PR/label state).

issue.description: Issue number the slot's golem owns.

branch.description: Git head branch of the slot's golem.

backlog_depth.description: Cached count of eligible backlog issues (priority order, status-label-excluded) at the last sweep. DISPLAY ONLY; the live backlog is re-queried each refill.

updated.description: ISO datetime of the last pool.json write.
